summary: Matched a large block of ftCo_Attack100.c, replacing many nonmatching stubs with C implementations for Attack100 rapid-jab flow plus nearby item pickup, item scope, jump aerial F1, and catch-dash helpers. The decomp.dev bot reported 41 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100 and +3516 matched bytes, raising matched code by +0.09%. The PR also exposed needed cross-file prototypes and refined fighter/common structs so these implementations could reference ftpickupitem helpers, ftData special callbacks, motion vars, and Fighter_x2D0 fields.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed ftCo_Attack100.c functions, including ftCo_800D67C4, ftCo_800D6824, ftCo_800D688C, ftCo_800D68C0, ftCo_800D6928, ftCo_Attack100_CheckInput, ftCo_800D69C4, ftCo_Attack_800D6A50, fn_800D6AC4, ftCo_800D6B00, fn_800D6B8C, ftCo_Attack100Start_Anim, ftCo_800D6C60, ftCo_Attack100Loop_Anim, ftCo_Attack100End_Anim, Attack100 Phys/Coll callbacks, fn_800D6F58, ftCo_800D705C, ftCo_800D7100, ftCo_800D71D8, ftCo_JumpAerialF1 callbacks, several ItemScope callbacks, fn_800D8BFC, and ftCo_CatchDash_Anim.
Added rapid-jab state tracking through mv.co.attack100.x0 and mv.co.attack100.x4 in ftCommon motion vars.
Added Game & Watch-specific routing in fn_800D6AC4: FTKIND_GAMEWATCH calls ftGw_Attack100Start_Enter, while other fighters enter the common Attack100Start path.

Matched Attack100 loop/end logic around cur_anim_frame, frame_speed_mul, throw_flags_b3, repeated A input, ftpickupitem_80094790 checks, and Fighter_ChangeMotionState to ftCo_MS_Attack100End or ftCo_MS_Attack100Loop.

Implemented item pickup helper behavior for Attack100-adjacent code: fn_800D6F58 attaches item_gobj, chooses FtPart_TopN for It_Kind_WStar or the fighter data part otherwise, calls pl_8003E854, Item_8026AB54, ftpickupitem_8009447C, spawns efAsync effect 0x422, and sets x2224_b1.

Made several ftpickupitem functions externally visible by moving declarations from static prototypes in ftpickupitem.c to ftpickupitem.h: ftpickupitem_800942A0, ftpickupitem_8009447C, ftpickupitem_80094694, and ftpickupitem_800948A8.
Added ftData_SpecialN, ftData_SpecialLw, and ftData_SpecialHi extern declarations to ftdata.h so Attack100 input checks can dispatch character special callbacks.

Refined struct typing in ft/types.h: ftCommonData.x414 changed from UNK_T to int, ftData.x8->x10 documented as Fighter_Part, Fighter.x2D0 became a typed struct pointer with int fields x0/x4/x8 and float fields xC/x10, and Fighter.x209C changed from s16 to u16.
Added header prototypes for newly used local callbacks and helpers in ftCo_Attack100.h, and changed several return types from s32 to bool where the implementation returns true/false.
smart_moves: Used small wrapper callbacks for repeated state-machine patterns: ftCo_800D6C60 accepts an HSD_GObjEvent callback, and ItemScope IASA wrappers pass function pointers into fn_800D79B4 instead of duplicating behavior.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack layout with PAD_STACK(4) and PAD_STACK(8) in small functions such as fn_800D6B8C, ftCo_Attack100Loop_Anim, ftCo_JumpAerialF1_Anim, and ftCo_CatchDash_Anim.

Converted unknown or overly broad types to semantically useful types only where needed for matching and correctness: bool return values, enum_t motion IDs, u16 x209C, typed Fighter_x2D0_t, and Item_GObj/Fighter_GObj parameters.
Lifted ftpickupitem helper prototypes out of file-local static declarations only after ftCo_Attack100.c needed to call them, avoiding ad hoc externs in the C file.
Handled character-specific behavior minimally by branching only FTKIND_GAMEWATCH to ftGw_Attack100Start_Enter and leaving the common rapid-jab path for the default case.
Matched boolean gate patterns explicitly, including temporary bool variables for combined input/item conditions and throw_flags_b3 consumption, which likely helped reproduce compiler control flow.
Kept motion callback implementations simple by delegating to existing common physics/collision helpers such as ft_80084FA8, ft_80084104, ftCo_800CDE54, ftCo_800CDE74, ft_800841B8, and ft_80082C74.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter common action touches item logic, move real helper prototypes into the relevant header rather than leaving them static in the implementation file; this enables cross-unit matching without unsafe local declarations.
Rapid-jab matching needs explicit motion-variable layout: a two-bool attack100 struct at fp+2340/fp+2344 was required for loop/end state and A-input continuation logic.

Special-move input checks can be matched as callback-table guards: verify ftData_SpecialN/Lw/Hi entry is non-NULL, check the per-fighter lockout field such as x689/x687/x686, call the function pointer, and return true.
For common fighter files, matching often depends on small type refinements in broad structs, especially signedness and typed nested pointers used by nearby callbacks.

Item pickup decomp can require both gameplay state and effects: item attachment, player/item bookkeeping, joint selection, pickup helper calls, efAsync_Spawn, and bit flags all appear in one matched helper.
Motion callbacks often reduce to one existing engine helper; do not overcomplicate Phys/Coll stubs when the assembly likely just tail-calls or directly calls common helper routines.
No human review comments were present in the slice, so lessons are inferred from the diff and decomp.dev match report rather than reviewer guidance.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action functions retain the ftCo_ prefix, e.g. ftCo_Attack100Loop_Anim, ftCo_Attack100Start_Phys, and ftCo_CatchDash_Anim.
Unidentified static/local helpers keep address-based fn_ names, e.g. fn_800D6AC4, fn_800D6F58, fn_800D8BFC.
Address comments were maintained in headers for function declarations, e.g. /* 0D6F58 */ and /* 0942A0 */.
Motion callback suffixes follow the standard Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll naming pattern.
ftpickupitem symbols keep the ftpickupitem_8009xxxx address-style names until semantic names are known.
Fighter motion variable fields remain offset-style x0/x4 when semantics are not yet established, nested under mv.co.attack100.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK to force stack shape in functions that otherwise have very small bodies or compiler-sensitive locals.
Declared temporaries such as bool var_r0 and bool tmp to match branch/control-flow shape for boolean conditions.
Used ABS from math.h for stick magnitude checks in ftCo_800D67C4.
Used direct float literals with F suffix in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls: 0.0F, 1.0F, 0.0F.
Used NULL checks and early returns around function pointer tables and item pointers to match simple assembly guard patterns.
Preserved field access style differences where present: both GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and gobj->user_data appear in matched functions.
Introduced a typed Fighter_x2D0_t so ftCo_JumpAerialF1_Phys could express xC and x10 float multipliers cleanly.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 44.73%, +0.09%, with +3516 bytes and 41 new matches for main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename mv.co.attack100.x0 or x4 to semantic rapid-jab field names.
Search for subsequent matches in the remaining stubbed ftCo_Attack100.c functions such as ftCo_800D7268, ftCo_800D74A4, fn_800D79B4, and ItemScope rapid/fire callbacks.
Check whether ftData_SpecialAirHi declarations were later added near ftData_SpecialHi, since ftCo_800D69C4 calls ftData_SpecialAirHi but this diff excerpt only shows SpecialN/Lw/Hi additions.
Look for later cleanup of ftpickupitem_80094694 prototype types from int,int to FtMotionId,bool once cross-file matching constraints are understood.
Search for other fighter common PRs that use PAD_STACK in small motion callbacks for matching.
